title: Exact non-instruction configuration shape for .claude/launch.json
description: The ONLY file under a tool-local instruction directory that the instruction boundary may accept. It is accepted because of this schema, not because of its name. Every field is a bounded identifier, a bounded shell-safe token, a port number or a bounded host URL: `additionalProperties: false` at every level plus these character classes mean the document cannot express a sentence, a prompt, a persona, a rule, a system message or an instruction override. CP1-A.2 audit F3: `name` and `runtimeArgs` previously admitted spaces and shell metacharacters, which was wide enough to carry natural-language text.

name.description: An identifier or label. No whitespace, so a sentence cannot be spelled here.

runtimeExecutable.description: A command name or a repository-relative path, optionally written with a leading './'. No whitespace and no shell metacharacter.

runtimeArgs.description: Individual argv entries, never a shell command line. The character class excludes whitespace, quotes, and every shell metacharacter ($ ` ; | & > < ( ) { } [ ] * ? ! ~ ^ \ # ' "), so a pipeline, a command substitution, a redirection, a chained command and a natural-language sentence are all unrepresentable. Each entry is at most 64 characters.

url.description: Origin only. The class excludes '@', so user-info credentials cannot be embedded, and excludes every shell metacharacter.
